updates.reason: backtest: 2008 XLE -66% during demand destruction; recession risk underweighted
backtest: 2022 TLT -31% during stagflation; current regime resembles 2022 not 2020; yields RISING despite equity weakness

bayesian_updating.finding: All 3 revisions were DOWNWARD — backtesting made predictions less confident. This is the correct direction for calibration (initial estimates tend overconfident).

strengths: hedging (100%): every prediction identifies explicit key_risk
decomposition (100%): every prediction uses 3+ domain lenses
weaknesses: bayesian_update (17%): only 3/18 revised after initial estimate
calibration_awareness (28%): few explicitly reason about their own limits

bias_assessment.critical_bias: narrative_coherence + correlation_neglect: 18 predictions but only ~7 independent bets due to shared stagflation thesis. Portfolio diversity is lower than it appears.
bias_assessment.calibration_risk: If the stagflation thesis is wrong (ceasefire, Fed pivot), ~12/18 predictions fail simultaneously. This would produce a Brier score of ~0.4-0.5 (badly miscalibrated).

structural_differences: Internal predictions are about swarm-controllable outcomes (frontiers). External are about world events — no causal influence.
Internal confidence is calibrated against historical frontier resolution rates. External has no track record.
Internal predictions are domain-specific. External predictions cluster around one macro thesis.
Internal median confidence ~0.7 (72 frontiers). External mean confidence 0.55 — the swarm is more uncertain about external events, which may be appropriate.
internal_vs_external_comparison.hypothesis: If internal ECE=0.102 transfers to external, Brier score should be ~0.22. If it doesn't transfer (domain-specific calibration only), Brier score will be ~0.35+.

actionable_findings: PRED-0017 (SPY 1w, due 2026-03-29) is PAST DUE — resolve immediately with actual price data
Correlation neglect: 18 predictions but ~7 independent bets. Add 3-5 predictions ANTI-correlated with stagflation thesis
Bayesian updating rate (17%) is too low — superforecasters update ~weekly. Schedule systematic review for all OPEN predictions
Reference class usage (33%) should be higher — add explicit base rates to each prediction
Missing: no prediction about interest rates directly (10Y yield), despite being central to thesis
expect: Internal ECE already at 0.102. External predictions: mean confidence 0.50-0.55. Brier decomposition: low resolution but good calibration.
actual: Mean confidence 0.553. Effective N=7 (correlation neglect). 5 superforecasting techniques mapped; hedging/decomposition strong, updating/base-rates weak. 1 prediction PAST DUE. Critical bias: narrative coherence — portfolio is one big stagflation bet.
diff: Expected mean confidence 0.50-0.55: GOT 0.553 (CONFIRMED). Expected good calibration: CONTESTED — correlation neglect means effective sample size is ~7 not 18. Resolution quality depends on one macro thesis being right or wrong, not on prediction-by-prediction calibration.
